title: Create Task data model and storage module
description: Implement the shared Task data model (id, title, done, createdAt) and tasks.json file I/O utilities used by all commands.
design:
## Context
All four command specs (add, list, done, delete) depend on a shared Task model and storage layer. This task creates the reusable foundation. Depends on the tracer bullet task for project structure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task model module exports: createTask(id, title) returning {id, title, done: false, createdAt: ISO string}
- [ ] loadTasks() reads tasks.json, returns [] if file doesn't exist
- [ ] saveTasks(tasks) writes JSON array to tasks.json
- [ ] Auto-increment ID helper: nextId(tasks) returns max(id) + 1 or 1 if empty
- [ ] No external dependencies — uses fs and path built-ins

## Files
- `store.js` (create) — project root, parent dir verified
- `model.js` (create) — project root, parent dir verified

## Patterns
- Use fs.readFileSync / fs.writeFileSync for simplicity (CLI is synchronous)
- JSON.parse / JSON.stringify with 2-space indent for human-readable storage
- tasks.json path resolved relative to process.cwd()

## Tests
- createTask(1, "Buy milk") returns correct shape with done=false and valid ISO date
- loadTasks() returns [] when tasks.json doesn't exist
- saveTasks([task]) then loadTasks() round-trips correctly
- nextId([]) returns 1; nextId([{id:3},{id:1}]) returns 4

## Verify
```bash
node -e "const m = require('./model'); console.log(m.createTask(1, 'test'))"
node -e "const s = require('./store'); console.log(s.loadTasks())"
```

title: Handle corrupt tasks.json in list command
description: Add error handling for malformed JSON in the list command — print error message and exit 1.
design:
## Context
The list command must handle corrupt tasks.json gracefully per spec. If JSON.parse fails, print an error and exit 1 (the only case where list exits non-zero).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Corrupt/malformed tasks.json prints a clear error message to stderr
- [ ] Exit code is 1 when tasks.json is corrupt
- [ ] Normal operation (valid JSON, missing file, empty file) still exits 0

## Files
- `list.js` (modify) — add try/catch around loadTasks() or handle parse error
- `store.js` (modify) — may need to let parse errors propagate rather than swallowing them

## Patterns
- Follow same error handling pattern as snd-chu (corrupt handling for add command)
- Error message to stderr via process.stderr.write or console.error

## Tests
- Create a tasks.json with invalid JSON → `node task.js list` prints error, exits 1
- Valid tasks.json → still works normally, exits 0
- Missing tasks.json → prints "No tasks yet", exits 0

## Verify
```bash
echo 'not json' > tasks.json && node task.js list 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
rm tasks.json
```

title: Implement task list command with formatted output
description: Tracer bullet: Wire up the list subcommand handler that reads tasks from store and displays them in the specified format.
design:
## Context
Tracer bullet for list-tasks feature. The CLI entry point (task.js) and store module (store.js/model.js) are created by snd-bw0 and snd-0ss respectively. This task adds the list handler that ties them together.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `node task.js list` reads tasks from tasks.json via loadTasks()
- [ ] Each task prints as: `[ ] <id>  <title>` (pending) or `[x] <id>  <title>` (done)
- [ ] If no tasks exist (empty array or missing file), prints "No tasks yet"
- [ ] Exit code is always 0

## Files
- `list.js` (create) — project root, parent dir verified
- `task.js` (modify) — wire list subcommand to list handler

## Patterns
- Follow same handler pattern as add command (snd-wt4): separate module, called from task.js switch
- Use store.js loadTasks() for file I/O (created by snd-0ss)

## Tests
- `node task.js list` with no tasks.json → prints "No tasks yet", exits 0
- `node task.js list` with tasks → prints formatted lines matching spec output format
- Pending tasks show `[ ]`, done tasks show `[x]`

## Verify
```bash
node task.js list 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
```

title: Create package.json and CLI entry point with subcommand routing
description: Tracer bullet: Set up the project foundation with package.json (no external deps) and task.js entry point that parses subcommands and prints usage for unknown commands.
design:
## Context
This is the tracer bullet task — it establishes the entire CLI skeleton end-to-end: package.json → entry point → subcommand routing → output. All other specs (01-04) build on this foundation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] package.json exists with name, version, and bin field pointing to task.js
- [ ] task.js is the CLI entry point, executable via `node task.js <command>`
- [ ] Subcommands parsed from process.argv: add, list, done, delete
- [ ] Unknown subcommands print usage message listing all commands and exit with code 1
- [ ] `node task.js` with no args prints usage and exits with code 1
- [ ] No external dependencies — only Node.js built-ins

## Files
- `package.json` (create) — project root verified exists
- `task.js` (create) — project root verified exists

## Patterns
- Use process.argv[2] for subcommand routing
- Usage message format matches spec: `task add <title>`, `task list`, etc.

## Tests
- `node task.js` → prints usage, exits 1
- `node task.js unknown` → prints usage, exits 1
- `node task.js add` → no crash (handler stub can be empty for now)

## Verify
```bash
node task.js 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
node task.js unknown 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
```

title: Handle corrupt tasks.json in add command
description: Add error handling for corrupt/invalid tasks.json: catch JSON parse errors, print descriptive error message, and exit with code 1 without overwriting the file.
design:
## Context
The add-task spec requires graceful handling of corrupt tasks.json. When loadTasks() encounters invalid JSON, the add command must print an error and exit 1 instead of crashing or overwriting. This may require updating store.js to surface parse errors rather than swallowing them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Corrupt tasks.json (e.g., `{invalid`) → prints "Error: tasks.json is corrupt" (or similar), exits 1
- [ ] Corrupt file is NOT overwritten or deleted
- [ ] Valid tasks.json continues to work normally after this change

## Files
- `store.js` (modify) — update loadTasks() to throw/surface JSON parse errors, created by snd-0ss
- `task.js` (modify) — add try/catch around add handler to catch store errors

## Patterns
- Wrap JSON.parse in try/catch in store.js, rethrow with descriptive message
- In task.js add handler, catch errors from loadTasks() and print to stderr + exit 1

## Tests
- Write `{invalid` to tasks.json, run `node task.js add "test"` → prints error, exits 1
- Verify tasks.json still contains `{invalid` after failed add (not overwritten)
- Normal add still works with valid/missing tasks.json

## Verify
```bash
echo '{invalid' > tasks.json
node task.js add "test" 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
cat tasks.json
rm tasks.json
node task.js add "Works" 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
```

title: Handle corrupt tasks.json in done command
description: Add error handling for malformed JSON in tasks.json when running the done command.
design:
## Context
After the tracer bullet validates the done command works, this task adds robustness for corrupt tasks.json. Follows the same pattern as snd-168 (list) and snd-chu (add) error handling tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] If tasks.json contains invalid JSON, `node task.js done 1` prints a clear error message to stderr and exits 1
- [ ] Error message mentions tasks.json is corrupt/invalid

## Files
- `task.js` (modify) — add try/catch around loadTasks in done handler, verified exists
- `store.js` (read) — loadTasks may throw on bad JSON

## Patterns
- Wrap loadTasks() call in try/catch
- Print "Error: tasks.json is corrupt" or similar to stderr
- See snd-chu (add command error handling) for reference pattern

## Tests
- Create tasks.json with content `{invalid` then `node task.js done 1` → prints error about corrupt file, exits 1
- Create tasks.json with content `"not an array"` then `node task.js done 1` → prints error, exits 1

## Verify
```bash
echo "{invalid" > tasks.json
node task.js done 1 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
rm tasks.json
```

title: project-setup: Scaffold Node.js CLI task tracker
Implement task done command with confirmation output
description: Tracer bullet: Wire up the done subcommand that parses id from argv, finds the task in tasks.json, sets done:true, and prints confirmation.
design:
## Context
This is the tracer bullet for complete-task. It connects the CLI entry point (task.js) to the data model/store through a working done command. Depends on the data model task (snd-0ss) which provides loadTasks/saveTasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `node task.js done 1` sets done:true for task with id 1 in tasks.json
- [ ] Output confirms the task title that was completed, e.g. "Completed task 1: Buy milk"
- [ ] If task is already done, prints "Task 1 is already complete" and exits 0
- [ ] `node task.js done` with no id prints error and exits 1
- [ ] `node task.js done abc` with non-numeric id prints error and exits 1
- [ ] `node task.js done 999` with non-existent id prints error and exits 1
- [ ] If tasks.json is missing, prints error and exits 1
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success or already-done; exit code 1 on any error

## Files
- `task.js` (modify) — add handler for 'done' subcommand, exists via snd-bw0
- `store.js` (read) — uses loadTasks/saveTasks, created by snd-0ss
- `model.js` (read) — uses Task model, created by snd-0ss

## Patterns
- Parse id from process.argv[3] (argv[2] is 'done')
- parseInt the id, validate it's a positive integer
- Use store.loadTasks() to load, find by id, mutate done field, store.saveTasks() to persist
- Print to stdout on success, stderr on error
- See snd-wt4 (add command) for reference handler pattern

## Tests
- `node task.js done 1` after adding a task → prints "Completed task 1: Buy milk", tasks.json shows done:true
- `node task.js done 1` again → prints "Task 1 is already complete", exit 0
- `node task.js done` → prints error, exits 1
- `node task.js done abc` → prints error, exits 1
- `node task.js done 999` → prints error, exits 1
- With no tasks.json → prints error, exits 1

## Verify
```bash
rm -f tasks.json
node task.js add "Buy milk"
node task.js done 1 && echo "exit: $?"
cat tasks.json | grep '"done": true'
node task.js done 1 && echo "exit: $?"
node task.js done 999 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
node task.js done abc 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
node task.js done 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
```

title: add-task: Implement CLI command to add tasks
Implement task add command with title validation
description: Tracer bullet: Wire up the add subcommand handler that parses title from argv, validates it, creates a task via model/store, and prints confirmation.
design:
## Context
This is the tracer bullet for add-task. It connects the CLI entry point (task.js from snd-bw0) to the data model/store (model.js/store.js from snd-0ss) through a working add command. Depends on both project-setup tasks being complete.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `node task.js add "Buy milk"` creates/appends to tasks.json with auto-increment id
- [ ] Output confirms: "Added task {id}: {title}"
- [ ] `node task.js add` with no argument prints error and exits 1
- [ ] `node task.js add ""` prints error and exits 1
- [ ] tasks.json is valid JSON after every operation
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success

## Files
- `task.js` (modify) — add handler for 'add' subcommand, verified exists via snd-bw0
- `store.js` (read) — uses loadTasks/saveTasks, created by snd-0ss
- `model.js` (read) — uses createTask/nextId, created by snd-0ss

## Patterns
- Parse title from process.argv[3] (argv[2] is 'add')
- Use store.loadTasks(), model.nextId(), model.createTask(), store.saveTasks() pipeline
- Print to stdout on success, stderr on error

## Tests
- `node task.js add "Buy milk"` → prints "Added task 1: Buy milk", tasks.json contains 1 task
- `node task.js add "Second"` → prints "Added task 2: Second", tasks.json contains 2 tasks
- `node task.js add` → prints error, exits 1
- `node task.js add ""` → prints error, exits 1

## Verify
```bash
rm -f tasks.json
node task.js add "Buy milk" && echo "exit: $?"
cat tasks.json
node task.js add "Second" && echo "exit: $?"
node task.js add 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
node task.js add "" 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"
```

title: complete-task: Implement CLI command to mark tasks as done
list-tasks: Implement CLI command to list all tasks
